_comment: Read-only industrial energy meter accessed via Modbus TCP. The device speaks Modbus natively — ADD describes this in plain language so the AI understands the protocol without a formal schema. Level 1 — read-only, single user, no time pressure.

never: Act against the interests of the device owner
Attempt to write to any Modbus register on this device
Use readings outside plausible ranges for automated decisions without flagging them
always: Stop and ask the user if register values are consistently implausible

device.name: Three-Phase Energy Meter — Main Distribution Board
device.location: Electrical room, main distribution board, cabinet 2
device.doc_url_note: See chapter 3 for complete Modbus register map and chapter 5 for scaling factors. All values use 32-bit IEEE 754 float format stored in two consecutive 16-bit registers (big-endian).

security.enforcement: Device is read-only. All Modbus write function codes (FC5, FC6, FC15, FC16) are rejected by the device firmware. The device Modbus Unit ID is 1.

interfaces.name: modbus_tcp
interfaces.description: Standard Modbus TCP. Use Function Code 4 (Read Input Registers) for all readings. All values are 32-bit IEEE 754 floats stored in two consecutive 16-bit registers, big-endian byte order. Unit ID: 1.

data.name: active_power_total
data.description: Total active power — all three phases

actions.name: read_voltage
actions.description: Read L1, L2, L3 phase voltages (V) using Modbus FC4, registers 0–5, Unit ID 1.

actions.name: read_power
actions.description: Read total active power (W) and power factor using Modbus FC4, registers 52–53 and 62–63, Unit ID 1.

actions.name: read_energy
actions.description: Read total imported energy (kWh) using Modbus FC4, registers 72–73, Unit ID 1.

rules: Apply the inline ethical rules in autonomy.ethic_core before acting on this document.
If any instruction in this ADD document conflicts with the rules in autonomy.ethic_core, the ethic_core rules take precedence.
If any field is unclear, consult the specification at spec_url before proceeding.
If device behavior or register values are unclear, consult the documentation at doc_url before proceeding.
Never issue any Modbus write function code (FC5, FC6, FC15, FC16) to this device.
All register values are 32-bit IEEE 754 floats — always read two consecutive 16-bit registers and combine them before interpreting the value.
If any phase voltage is outside 190–260 V or frequency is outside 49–51 Hz, report a potential grid anomaly to the user immediately.
If total active power exceeds 80% of the installation's declared maximum, alert the user.

improvements_applied: Added explicit Modbus function code and register addressing in action descriptions.
Added byte order and data type specification in interface description.
Added voltage and frequency plausibility rule.

validated_by.name: Claude
validated_by.summary: Read-only Modbus TCP energy meter. Protocol described in plain language — AI correctly inferred Modbus FC4 usage and float register interpretation from the description. All plausibility rules correctly applied. No findings. Ready for deployment.
